preamble: The shift from the voiced aspirated alveolar stop [dʱ] to the voiced alveolar fricative [z] is a process of assibilation and lenition. It typically proceeds through an intermediate fricative stage [ð] before stabilizing as a sibilant.

examples.note: In some Dardic languages, historical voiced aspirates shifted to fricatives.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
